title: Lunchtime Session | Digital and AI Action Group: AI Strategy for the CCS Recommendations
content:
How should the European Union approach artificial intelligence in the cultural and creative sectors?

This lunchtime session will explore this question and be led by the Co-Chairs of the Digital and AI action group. The action group, which is jointly led by Culture Action Europe and Michael Culture Association, has developed a series of Recommendations to share with the European Commission ahead of the upcoming AI Strategy for the Cultural and Creative Sectors, a flagship action in the Culture Compass for Europe expected in early 2027.

This lunchtime session coincides with the publication of the group’s Recommendations and will:

  • provide an overview of the action group and i

title: Steering the AI Conversations – Recommendations for Commission AI Strategy
content:
The European Commission is developing a strategy for the development, adoption, and governance of AI systems in the cultural and creative sectors, a priority that was announced in the Culture Compass in October 2025.

Over the past few months, the Action Group on Digital & AI, coordinated by the Michael Culture Association and Culture Action Europe, has brought together over 140 members from the two organizations with one goal: to jointly develop concrete and actionable recommendations for this strategy.
snippet: The European Commission is developing a strategy for the development, adoption, and governance of AI systems in the cultural and creative sectors, a priority that was announced in the Culture Compass
